name: Alto Peña Carnicera desde Mata de Alcántara — circular
region_intro: Hiking around Mata De Alcántara offers routes through a landscape shaped by the Tagus River, with paths leading to historical viewpoints and natural features. The region features a mix of riverine environments, open terrain, and elevated points providing panoramic views. Trails often incorporate elements like ancient structures and reservoir vistas, making for varied hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Mata De Alcántara The most popular hiking route is Balcón del Mundo Route, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail that takes 1 hour 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views from a prominent viewpoint. Another top favourite among local hikers is Menhir del Cabezo from the Mirador del Presa del Embalse de Alcántara — circular by Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata, a moderate 8.3 miles (13.3 km) path. This circular route explores the area around the Alcántara reservoir, passing a menhir. Local hikers also love the Starlings from Alcántara — circular by the Tagus River, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Hiking around Mata De Alcántara is defined by river paths along the Tagus, routes to historical sites like the Puente de Alcántara, and trails offering views over the Embalse de Alcántara reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate hikes. The routes in Mata De Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 30 hikers have used komoot to explore Mata De Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Mata De Alcántara?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Mata De Alcántara, offering a variety of experiences through riverine landscapes, open terrain, and elevated viewpoints. The region provides options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate hikes.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Yes, Mata De Alcántara offers several easy hiking trails perfect for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304621877/puente-de-alcantara-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Alcántara Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2-mile (5.2 km) route that allows you to explore the area without significant elevation changes.

Q: What kind of natural features and landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Hiking in Mata De Alcántara often features views of the Tagus River and the vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325564" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. You can also encounter historical structures like the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/795488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcántara Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt; and other ancient sites, providing a blend of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Yes, the region has several circular routes. A notable one is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304667220/menhir-del-cabezo-from-the-mirador-del-presa-del-embalse-de-alcantara-circular-by-ruta-de-la-canada-real-de-gata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Menhir del Cabezo from the Mirador del Presa del Embalse de Alcántara — circular by Ruta de la Cañada Real de Gata&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.3-mile (13.3 km) path that explores the reservoir area and passes a significant menhir.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Mata De Alcántara?
A: The trails in Mata De Alcántara are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.2 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical points of interest, and the scenic views over the Tagus River and reservoir.

Q: Are there any trails that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990823282/balcon-del-mundo-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Balcón del Mundo Route&lt;/a&gt; is a popular choice, offering prominent viewpoints with expansive vistas over the surrounding landscape, including the Tagus River valley.

Q: Can I find trails suitable for families with children?
A: Many of the easier routes in Mata De Alcántara are suitable for families. These trails typically have gentle gradients and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outdoor experience for all ages. Look for routes described as 'easy' for the most family-friendly options.

Q: Are there any places for wild swimming or natural pools near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region offers natural swimming spots. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5696756" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Cantera de Alcántara Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt;, which is a great place to cool off after a hike, especially during warmer months. There are also various picnic areas near water bodies like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6089256" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Picnic Area at La Quebrada Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Mata De Alcántara?
A: The best time for hiking in Mata De Alcántara is typically during the spring and autumn months. The weather is milder, making for more comfortable conditions to explore the trails. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can be damp.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1304656936/starlings-from-alcantara-circular-by-the-tagus-river" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Starlings from Alcántara — circular by the Tagus River&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate 10.4-mile (16.8 km) trail. This route takes you through extensive riverine landscapes and provides a more immersive experience in the region's natural environment.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Mata De Alcántara?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many of the hiking trails in Mata De Alcántara. However, it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check for any specific local regulations or signage on individual routes.

Q: What kind of historical sites can be explored near the hiking routes?
A: Beyond the famous Roman Bridge, you can discover other historical gems. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6226522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roco-Campofrío Palace (Alcántara)&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5582900" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María de Almocóvar Church&lt;/a&gt; are notable sites that offer insight into the region's rich past and can be visited in conjunction with your hikes.
tips: Mata de Alcántara is a municipality in Extremadura located a few kilometers from the border with Portugal. The town is surrounded by meadows and riverside forests, and has beautiful religious buildings.
About 3 km northwest of Mata de Alcántara you reach the incredible natural rock formation called Peña Carnicera, one of the most spectacular and interesting Extremaduran rock altars.
